articles.title: Peru's election: A battle for the Presidency amid political chaos and crime
articles.summary: With 35 candidates in the race, Peru is set to elect its 9th president in less than a decade. Amid rising corruption and crime, voters are left asking: Can this election finally break the cycle?

articles.title: Another Giant Leap Reminds Us How Small We Are
articles.summary: A mission that took four astronauts farther than any human has ever traveled in the history of mankind has made people feel a little trippy.

articles.title: Mamdani, Leaning Into ‘Sewer Socialism,’ Gets His Hands Dirty
articles.summary: The mayor has embraced the decades-old concept of “sewer socialism,” hoping to improve New Yorkers’ lives by focusing on public services.

articles.title: Opinion: A well-deserved statue for a hero rat
articles.summary: Cambodia is recognizing the life-saving contributions of a rat named Magawa with a statue. The late rat sniffed out landmines for a non-profit group, and in a short career helped find more than 100.

articles.title: What We Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Talks in Pakistan
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance is leading the U.S. delegation, which is in Pakistan for talks with Iranian officials on Saturday.

articles.title: China's Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of key summit with Trump
articles.summary: Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.

articles.title: The Great Green Wall's one of the world's most ambitious eco-projects. Is it working?
articles.summary: It's a global effort with a multibillion dollar price tag. Among its aims: re-greening nearly 250 million acres, planting 4,000 miles of trees, helping farmers, creating jobs, sequestering carbon.

articles.title: Gut troubles?  This gastroenterologist has tips to help you achieve 'poophoria'
articles.summary: In her new book You've Been Pooping All Wrong, Dr. Trisha Pasricha shares habits and practices to make your relationship with your solid waste as smooth as possible

articles.title: India cracks down on satirists for turning its prime minister into a punch line
articles.summary: India's satirists are turning Prime Minister Narendra Modi into a punch line — and the government is hitting back.

articles.title: After a whirlwind mission to the moon, astronauts are back home. Here's what's next
articles.summary: The Orion crew module containing the four Artemis II astronauts splashed down in the Pacific Ocean Friday evening.

articles.title: In Syria, instability allowed ISIS fighters to flee camps, but many of their families still detained
articles.summary: NPR visits the last detention camp for ISIS wives and children in an increasingly precarious northeastern Syria.

articles.title: Iran Tries to Grasp Economic Devastation of War, and Find a Way Past It
articles.summary: The vast scale of destruction wrought by U.S. and Israeli bombardment will make sanctions relief all the more vital to Iran’s government as it tries to negotiate a peace agreement.

articles.title: As Election Looms, Will Alito Retire, Giving Trump a New Supreme Court Pick?
articles.summary: Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. is the subject of intense speculation about whether he will retire in the coming months and give President Trump a fourth nominee.

articles.title: How AI is getting better at finding security holes
articles.summary: Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.

articles.title: Iran Has Been Consistent in War. Will It Be Consistent in Peace Talks?
articles.summary: While President Trump’s war aims have changed by the moment, Iran has stuck to firm demands. The question is whether it will compromise in peace negotiations.

articles.title: Pakistan hosts U.S.-Iran peace talks after weeks of frantic diplomacy
articles.summary: Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, is set to host peace talks today with leaders from Iran and the US, including Vice President J.D. Vance.

articles.title: Artemis II Splashdown Gives NASA Momentum in Renewed Moon Race
articles.summary: The astronauts — three Americans and one Canadian — captivated the world with their historic mission.

articles.title: How to Be Mayor in 100 Days: Mamdani Grapples With a Learning Curve
articles.summary: Zohran Mamdani has accrued a long list of quick accomplishments as mayor of New York City, but he has also abandoned some key campaign promises.

articles.title: Shortlisted for an Oscar, 'Homebound' is a daring movie about two dear friends
articles.summary: The movie, now streaming on Netflix, defied current trends in Indian cinema to tell the true story of a friendship between a Muslim and a Hindu Dalit. Martin Scorsese was secretly involved.

articles.title: Five things to know about Hungary's election
articles.summary: Hungary votes Sunday in a pivotal test of Viktor Orbán's "illiberal democracy," as challenger Péter Magyar taps voter frustration, with stakes for Europe, NATO and the U.S.

articles.title: VP Vance to lead U.S. team in Iran peace talks. And, Artemis II to return to Earth
articles.summary: Vice President Vance is heading to Pakistan to lead a U.S. team in talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. And, Artemis II is set to return to Earth today.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
